summary: Hidden click-zone counter games. Each zone advances a hidden counter on click; level passes when zone counters reach target. Same-action-class repetition is expected and correct; do not block on action class.
action_pattern: identify candidate zones from frame -> click one zone -> read observed_diff -> if minor_noise/no_change x3 in same zone -> switch zone
block_signals: zone clicked 3+ times with no progress
do_not_block_on: valid clicks in same zone before 3-strike threshold
hypothesis_rivalry: A: target zone is visually highlighted | B: any active zone advances counter | C: zone target depends on previous-level state

summary: One-shot ordered target sets. Each click selects a unique element; repeated clicks on same element typically waste actions.
action_pattern: enumerate remaining targets -> click one fresh target -> verify removal/state-change -> next fresh target
block_signals: click on already-selected element with no env change
hypothesis_rivalry: A: ordering matters | B: any order works | C: certain pairs must be co-selected

summary: Single click can simultaneously trigger multiple overlapping button sprites. Single-button BFS will undercount possibilities. Treat clicks as multi-button activations.
required_observations: button overlap map at click site
action_pattern: scan overlap regions -> prefer clicks that activate multiple goal-bearing buttons -> verify with observed_diff
hypothesis_rivalry: A: each button is independent | B: overlapping buttons fire together | C: order of overlap matters

summary: Four-direction movement on a grid; goal reached by shortest path after wall probing. Special action use must be justified.
action_pattern: probe walls minimally -> compute shortest path -> step along path -> if step blocked, re-probe local walls
hypothesis_rivalry: A: walls are static | B: walls toggle on entry | C: certain cells require special action

summary: Navigate close to a target before clicking. Click-before-navigate creates no-op clicks far from target.
required_observations: click validity at agent's current cell
action_pattern: move toward target until click is valid -> click -> verify state change
block_signals: click when far from any known target
hypothesis_rivalry: A: click is positional | B: click is global | C: click depends on adjacent tile state

summary: ACTION5 (or special) is the core activation cycle, not ordinary movement. Treating it as movement causes loops.
required_observations: situations where special action changes state
action_pattern: use special only at sites where prior probe showed it advances state -> elsewhere use movement
hypothesis_rivalry: A: special is location-gated | B: special is state-gated | C: special is timing-gated

summary: Score-mode games judge by score/state/terminal WIN, not the normal level counter. levels_completed metadata may mislead.
action_pattern: track score delta per action -> prefer actions with positive score evidence -> stop at terminal WIN
hypothesis_rivalry: A: score is monotonic | B: score has reset cycles | C: certain actions cap score

summary: Arrange via clicks, then ACTION5/commit. ACTION5 before layout completion is wasted.
action_pattern: clicks until layout matches expected pattern -> commit
hypothesis_rivalry: A: any complete layout commits | B: only specific layout commits | C: order of commit matters

summary: Click-zone grammar learned in early levels transfers to later walls/levels. Pure click or pure walk both fail.
action_pattern: learn zone grammar in early levels -> apply to later level walls
hypothesis_rivalry: A: grammar is global | B: grammar is per-level | C: grammar evolves with level
